openmeter/registry.purpose: Organisational folder owning the entitlement registry: a registry.Entitlement struct (entitlement.go) plus a non-Wire factory in builder/ that wires every entitlement-domain adapter, connector, and service into one struct for callers (balance-worker, test suites) that must not import app/common.

patterns.name: Registry struct + factory split
patterns.description: entitlement.go defines the field-only Entitlement struct; builder/ holds the sole factory GetEntitlementRegistry that populates every field. Callers never build the struct manually.

patterns.name: Every struct field must be populated by the factory
patterns.description: Adding a field to registry.Entitlement requires a matching assignment in builder/entitlement.go or downstream nil-derefs occur.

patterns.name: Interface-typed fields only
patterns.description: All fields are domain interface types (feature.FeatureConnector, credit.GrantConnector, entitlement.Service) — no concrete adapters leak through.

key_file_guides.role: Field-only Entitlement registry struct holding all entitlement sub-service interfaces.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a field without populating it in builder/entitlement.go causes runtime nil-dereferences.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common from this folder — it must stay independent to avoid import cycles with test suites and balance-worker
Putting wiring/factory logic in entitlement.go — wiring belongs only in builder/
Returning a partially-populated *registry.Entitlement from the factory

decisions.decision: Non-Wire single-function factory instead of a Wire provider set
decisions.rationale: balance-worker and test suites need entitlement infrastructure without the full app/common import graph; a standalone factory avoids import cycles while centralising wiring.

openmeter/secret.purpose: Manages encrypted secrets (e.g. Stripe API keys) for installed marketplace apps. The root package owns the Service and Adapter interfaces; sub-packages provide the stub plaintext adapter (adapter/), pure import-cycle-free domain types (entity/), and a thin validation-only service implementation (service/).

patterns.name: Adapter embeds SecretAdapter; Service embeds SecretService
patterns.description: New storage/service operations are added to the inner SecretAdapter/SecretService interface, never directly to the wrapping Adapter/Service.

patterns.name: Validate-then-delegate in service/
patterns.description: service/ calls input.Validate() unconditionally before delegating to the adapter; the stub adapter does no validation.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return secretentity.SecretID{}, err }; return s.adapter.CreateAppSecret(ctx, input)

patterns.name: Pure domain types in entity/ sub-package
patterns.description: SecretID, Secret, all input structs (each with Validate()), and SecretNotFoundError live in entity/ with zero Ent/persistence imports — this breaks the openmeter/app <-> openmeter/secret import cycle.

patterns.name: Stub adapter encodes plaintext value in SecretID.ID
patterns.description: The plaintext adapter stores the raw secret value as SecretID.ID — a known stub convention until a real secret store is wired; CreateAppSecret/DeleteAppSecret always succeed.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Adapter/SecretAdapter interfaces consumed by the service layer.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding persistence logic here — storage belongs in adapter sub-packages.
New methods must be added to SecretService first, then implemented in service/.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic or cross-domain calls to service/ — it is validation + delegation only
Injecting *entdb.Client into Service — persistence is the adapter's concern
Importing openmeter/ent/db in entity/ — it must stay a pure domain-type package to preserve the cycle break
Returning errors from the stub adapter's Create/Delete — callers expect them to succeed

decisions.decision: Input types live in a separate entity/ sub-package
decisions.rationale: Keeps the root package to pure interfaces and lets adapter and service import input types without circular dependencies.

decisions.decision: Plaintext stub adapter stores value in SecretID.ID
decisions.rationale: Lets the app run without a real secret store in development; a production adapter can replace it without changing the Adapter interface.

openmeter/server.purpose: Chi-based HTTP server assembling the v1 and v3 REST APIs behind a shared middleware stack (auth, OpenAPI validation, CORS, logging). server/ root owns Chi router construction, dual-version mounting, and RFC 7807 error mapping; the router/ sub-package is the pure v1 endpoint delegation layer implementing api.ServerInterface.

patterns.name: Dual-version route registration in NewServer
patterns.description: NewServer mounts v3 via v3server.NewServer + RegisterRoutes in its own Chi Group first, then v1 via api.HandlerWithOptions in a separate Group so each version has its own middleware chain and validator.
patterns.example: r.Group(func(r chi.Router){ v3RegisterErr = v3API.RegisterRoutes(r) }); api.HandlerWithOptions(impl, api.ChiServerOptions{BaseRouter: r, Middlewares: middlewares})

patterns.name: router.Config aggregates every domain service; validated before routing
patterns.description: config.RouterConfig holds ~40 domain service interface fields; router.NewRouter validates them before the server is constructed. v3 wiring reads the same fields from RouterConfig.

patterns.name: StaticNamespaceDecoder injected universally
patterns.description: namespacedriver.StaticNamespaceDecoder(defaultNS) is passed into both v1 router.Config and v3server.Config — namespace is never resolved inside a handler.

patterns.name: RFC 7807 error responses via models.NewStatusProblem
patterns.description: NotFound, MethodNotAllowed, and the errorHandlerReply oapi-codegen error switch all render application/problem+json via models.NewStatusProblem.

patterns.name: v1 validator runs with NoopAuthenticationFunc + ExcludeReadOnlyValidations
patterns.description: OapiRequestValidatorWithOptions uses NoopAuthenticationFunc (auth handled by the authenticator middleware) and ExcludeReadOnlyValidations so read-only zero-values are not rejected.

patterns.name: corsHandler with AllowedPaths prefix gate
patterns.description: CORS applies only to path prefixes in corsOptions.AllowedPaths (e.g. /api/v1/portal/meters); empty AllowedPaths means CORS for all paths.

key_file_guides.role: NewServer: builds the Chi router, wires v1+v3 middleware stacks, mounts both API versions, and defines errorHandlerReply.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Middleware order — authenticator must precede OapiRequestValidator; no business/domain logic here.
Empty/nil AllowedPaths enables CORS for all paths — intentional only for the portal use case.

key_file_guides.role: TestRoutes smoke test exercising v1 and v3 routes against an in-memory server built from mocks.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New endpoints should add a case here to verify routing and response shape.

key_file_guides.role: Unit test for ValidationIssue -> HTTP status mapping through httptransport/commonhttp.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Changes to commonhttp.HandleIssueIfHTTPStatusKnown must be reflected here.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic, DB calls, or domain service calls in server.go — logic belongs in domain httpdriver packages and the router delegation layer
Registering v3 routes inside the v1 Chi Group — each version needs its own group and middleware chain
Skipping models.NewStatusProblem for error responses — all errors must render as application/problem+json
Hand-editing api/api.gen.go or api/v3/api.gen.go to add routes — regenerate from TypeSpec via make gen-api

decisions.decision: v3 mounted in a separate Chi Group before the v1 group
decisions.rationale: Each version needs its own validator (v3 oasmiddleware.ValidateRequest; v1 kin-openapi OapiRequestValidatorWithOptions); a shared group would mix validator instances.

decisions.decision: ExcludeReadOnlyValidations: true in the v1 OpenAPI filter
decisions.rationale: Go models translate required+readOnly fields to non-nil zero values; excluding read-only validation prevents false rejections on fields that should not appear in requests.

openmeter/streaming.purpose: Defines the streaming.Connector interface (ClickHouse-backed usage querying and event ingestion), all query-parameter types with Validate(), and the Customer/CustomerUsageAttribution attribution model. Concrete implementation lives in clickhouse/, a transient-retry decorator in retry/, and an in-memory test double in testutils/.

patterns.name: Connector embeds namespace.Handler
patterns.description: streaming.Connector embeds namespace.Handler so every implementation participates in namespace lifecycle (CreateNamespace/DeleteNamespace) alongside query/ingest methods.
patterns.example: type Connector interface { namespace.Handler; QueryMeter(...); BatchInsert(...) }

patterns.name: Validate() on every Params type
patterns.description: QueryParams, ListEventsParams, ListEventsV2Params, ListSubjectsParams, ListGroupByValuesParams each implement Validate(); callers validate before calling the connector.
patterns.example: if err := params.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: Customer interface + CustomerUsageAttribution value type
patterns.description: Any type implementing GetUsageAttribution() can be a QueryParams.FilterCustomer; CustomerUsageAttribution is the concrete value with Validate()/GetValues()/Equal().

patterns.name: FilterStoredAt uses FilterTimeUnix (second precision)
patterns.description: stored_at predicates use *filter.FilterTimeUnix, not *filter.FilterTime, because ClickHouse DateTime columns are second-precision.

patterns.name: Query-struct with toSQL() (in clickhouse/)
patterns.description: Every query type in the clickhouse/ sub-package is a struct with a toSQL() method built via sqlbuilder; no raw SQL in Connector method bodies.
patterns.example: q := meterQuery{namespace: ns, meter: m, params: p}; sql, args := q.toSQL()

key_file_guides.role: Connector interface plus CountEventsParams, RawEvent, ListSubjectsParams, ListGroupByValuesParams with inline Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a method here without a matching implementation in clickhouse/, retry/, and testutils/.

key_file_guides.role: QueryParams with customer_id group-by constraints (requires FilterCustomer when groupBy=customer_id; max 1 FilterCustomer otherwise).
key_file_guides.watch_for: New group-by dimensions added without updating the Validate() constraint.

key_file_guides.role: ListEventsParams (From required) and cursor-based ListEventsV2Params; EventSortField.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Conflating the two listing APIs — v1 uses From, v2 uses Cursor.

key_file_guides.role: Customer interface and CustomerUsageAttribution value type; GetValues() feeds ClickHouse IN clauses.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CustomerUsageAttribution.Validate() requires at least Key or one SubjectKey.

anti_patterns: Putting raw SQL or fmt.Sprintf interpolation in Connector methods or clickhouse/ — use the query-struct toSQL() with sqlbuilder.Var()
Wrapping BatchInsert with retry in retry/ — duplicate inserts break idempotency
Adding application errors (MeterNotFoundError) to the retry predicate — only transient infra errors retry
Using filter.FilterTime for stored_at predicates instead of filter.FilterTimeUnix
Returning raw ClickHouse errors — map code 60 to NamespaceNotFoundError/MeterNotFoundError

decisions.decision: Single shared events table across namespaces with namespace as leading ORDER BY column
decisions.rationale: Simplifies schema management; the leading namespace sort key gives efficient per-namespace partition pruning without per-namespace DDL.

decisions.decision: Query-struct toSQL() pattern instead of inline SQL
decisions.rationale: Each query type becomes independently testable via SQL-string assertions and avoids inline interpolation bugs.

decisions.decision: store_row_id (ULID) as the v2 cursor tie-breaker
decisions.rationale: Timestamps are not unique at millisecond granularity; a sortable ULID provides a monotonic secondary key without a new column.

openmeter/taxcode.purpose: Manages tax codes (with per-app-type mappings) used during invoice line processing. The root package owns the Service and Repository interfaces, input/output types, and ValidationIssue error sentinels; adapter/ provides Ent/PostgreSQL persistence; service/ provides business logic (system-managed protection, get-or-create with conflict retry); testutils/ wires a full stack without app/common.

patterns.name: ValidationIssue sentinels in errors.go
patterns.description: Every domain error is a package-level models.ValidationIssue with an ErrorCode, HTTP status attribute, and field path; constructor helpers (NewTaxCodeNotFoundError, NewTaxCodeByAppMappingNotFoundError) attach context attrs.

patterns.name: Validate() + compile-time models.Validator assertion on every input
patterns.description: Each input struct implements models.Validator with a var _ models.Validator = (*XInput)(nil) assertion in service.go.

patterns.name: transaction.Run at service layer + TransactingRepo in adapter
patterns.description: Service methods wrap adapter calls in transaction.Run for atomicity; each adapter method wraps Ent access with entutils.TransactingRepo so the ctx-bound tx is honoured.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.repo, func(ctx context.Context) (TaxCode, error) { return s.repo.CreateTaxCode(ctx, input) })

patterns.name: IsManagedBySystem guard with AllowAnnotations bypass
patterns.description: Before Update/Delete the service checks tc.IsManagedBySystem() and returns ErrTaxCodeManagedBySystem unless input.AllowAnnotations (from embedded inputOptions) is true.

patterns.name: JSONB containment query via raw sql.Selector
patterns.description: GetTaxCodeByAppMapping uses a raw sql.P/sql.Selector with the JSONB @> operator for app-mapping lookups, not a standard Ent predicate.

key_file_guides.role: Service interface, all input types with Validate(), compile-time Validator assertions, inputOptions{AllowAnnotations}.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New inputs without Validate()/assertions; new mutating methods skipping the IsManagedBySystem guard.

key_file_guides.role: TaxCode domain type, TaxCodeAppMapping(s) with uniqueness validation, IsManagedBySystem, GetAppMapping.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TaxCodeStripeRegexp (txcd_\d{8}) must stay in sync with Stripe's tax code format.

key_file_guides.role: ValidationIssue sentinels + ErrorCode constants + IsTaxCodeNotFoundError predicate.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returning raw errors instead of sentinels breaks HTTP status mapping.
Service methods added without a matching Repository method compile but fail at runtime.

key_file_guides.role: namespace.Handler implementation for taxcode namespace lifecycle.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Namespace provisioning side-effects must stay idempotent.

anti_patterns: Calling adapter methods outside transaction.Run in the service layer — loses atomicity for get-then-mutate flows
Skipping the IsManagedBySystem check in new mutating methods — lets external callers overwrite system-synced records
Implementing business logic (key derivation, conflict retry) in the adapter instead of the service layer
Adding raw SQL outside a sql.P/sql.Selector wrapper in the adapter — bypasses Ent's query builder
Importing app/common in testutils/ — creates import cycles and couples test setup to the full DI graph

decisions.decision: transaction.Run at the service layer in addition to TransactingRepo in the adapter
decisions.rationale: Service-level transaction.Run keeps multi-step operations (get-or-create with retry) atomic; adapter-level TransactingRepo keeps individual DB calls on the ctx-bound transaction even when called directly.
System-sync processes must update system-managed tax codes without relaxing the public API; a typed flag keeps the bypass explicit and auditable.
